title: mod_perl Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for mod_perl usage.

commands.scenario: Use when you need to activate mod_perl for your Apache server.
Use after enabling mod_perl to apply changes.

commands.description: Configure a Perl handler for a specific location
commands.scenario: Use to define a Perl script handler for a specific URL path.

commands.description: Enable warnings and taint mode for Perl scripts
commands.scenario: Use to enforce safe coding practices in your Perl scripts.

commands.description: Load a Perl module at server startup
commands.scenario: Use to preload a Perl module for performance optimization.

commands.description: Execute a Perl script at server startup
commands.scenario: Use to run initialization code when the server starts.

commands.description: Set a Perl variable for use in scripts
commands.scenario: Use to define custom variables accessible in your Perl scripts.

commands.description: Run Perl scripts under mod_perl's Registry mode
commands.scenario: Use to execute Perl scripts with mod_perl's Registry handler for faster execution.

commands.command:
<FilesMatch \.pl$>
    SetHandler perl-script
    PerlResponseHandler ModPerl::Registry
    Options +ExecCGI
    PerlOptions +ParseHeaders
</FilesMatch>
commands.scenario: Use to ensure .pl files are executed by mod_perl.

commands.description: Enable parent-child process model for mod_perl
commands.scenario: Use to improve performance by sharing memory between processes.

commands.description: Set a cleanup handler to run after requests
commands.scenario: Use to perform cleanup tasks after a request is processed.

commands.description: Set a handler to run after the request is read
commands.scenario: Use to process data immediately after a request is read.
